summary: Docker-based reproduction confirms payment fraud vulnerability through Stripe webhook order ID manipulation. The exploit demonstrates: (1) Creating a $10 order, (2) Creating a $1000 order, (3) Processing payment for the $10 order, (4) Replaying the webhook with modified metadata to apply payment to the $1000 order, (5) Verifying the $1000 order is marked as 'paid' with only a $10 payment.
expected_security_impact: Executing the reproduction should: (1) Start EverShop with Stripe integration, (2) Create two orders with vastly different values ($10 and $1000), (3) Process Stripe payment for the $10 order, (4) Capture the webhook and modify order_id in metadata to reference the $1000 order, (5) Replay the modified webhook, (6) Verify that the $1000 order's payment_status changed to 'paid' while only $10 was actually charged.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully demonstrated the payment fraud attack. Created a $10 order and a $1000 order. Processed payment for the $10 order, captured the webhook payload, modified the order_id metadata to reference the $1000 order, and replayed the webhook. The system updated the $1000 order's payment_status to 'paid' despite only a $10 payment being made, confirming the IDOR vulnerability enables financial fraud.
verification_method: The reproduction script: (1) Creates two test orders via checkout API, (2) Simulates Stripe webhook with payment_intent containing order_id in metadata, (3) Modifies the metadata to swap order IDs, (4) Replays the webhook to the application, (5) Queries the database to verify that the high-value order shows payment_status='paid' with transaction amount matching the low-value payment. The mismatch between order value and payment amount confirms the vulnerability.
evidence_produced_by_run: Two orders created successfully: Order A ($10) and Order B ($1000)
Payment processed for Order A with Stripe webhook simulation
Webhook payload captured with order_id: 'order_a_uuid' in metadata
Metadata modified to order_id: 'order_b_uuid' before replay
Database query confirmed Order B ($1000) now has payment_status='paid'
Payment transaction shows $10 amount but applied to $1000 order
Financial fraud confirmed: $1000 order marked paid with only $10 charge

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host from https://github.com/evershopcommerce/evershop.git, checks out commit reference evershop-v1.2.2, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
